home.heroSubtitle: Choose an observatory, pick a date, and complete your registration. The whole process takes less than 3 minutes.
home.paranalDesc: In 2024 it detected 32 erupting protostars and captured supernova SN 2024ggi just 26 hours after detection. The first observatory to directly photograph an exoplanet (2004).
home.lasillaDesc: HARPS, the world's most precise spectrograph for detecting exoplanets, operates here. In December 2025, the new SOXS instrument made its first observations, opening the study of rapid astrophysical transient events.
home.groupsBody: University courses, schools, corporate visits — up to 40 spots at La Silla and up to 60 spots at Paranal. Group visits run on Saturdays during winter season. Download the template, fill in the group details and send it requesting your date. The ESO team will coordinate directly with you.
home.groupsBodySilla: up to 40 spots at La Silla
home.step1Desc: Select above. You'll see the calendar with available dates and time slots in real time.
home.step2Desc: Enter your details and those of your companions (max. 10 people). You'll receive a confirmation by email.
home.step3Desc: You'll receive an automatic reminder. Confirm before Friday 12:00 to secure your spot.
home.cap1Desc: See whether your booking is pending confirmation, confirmed, or cancelled.
home.cap2Desc: Add or remove people from your group. Each change resets the confirmation deadline.
home.cap3Desc: Confirm you'll attend before Friday 12:00. Without confirmation, your spot is released.
home.cap4Desc: Cancel if your plans change. Only possible before the Friday prior to your visit.
home.deadlineTitle: Deadline: the Friday before your visit, 12:00 (Santiago time)
home.deadlineBody: After that moment, the system automatically closes the modification window. Only the ESO team can make changes after that time. If you don't confirm, your spot is released to other visitors.
home.faq1q: How many people can I include in an individual booking?
home.faq1a: Maximum 10 people. If your group exceeds that number (courses, schools, corporate visits), use the group booking flow: download the template from the observatories section and request your visit through the group form.
home.faq2q: How long can I modify or cancel?
home.faq2a: Until the Friday before your visit date, before 12:00 Santiago time. After that moment, the system automatically closes the window. Only the ESO team can make changes after that time.
home.faq3q: What happens if I don't confirm my attendance?
home.faq3a: The system automatically cancels your booking and releases the spots to other visitors. You'll receive an email reminder before the deadline. Confirmation is mandatory and must be done from the My Booking portal with your ESO-XXXXXXXX code.
home.faq4q: Can I add or remove companions after booking?
home.faq4a: Yes. From My Booking you can add people if there are spots available in the time slot, or remove companions at any time (no spot restriction for removals). Each modification resets the confirmation status to Pending.
home.faq5q: Is there a minimum age for visits?
home.faq5a: At Paranal: minimum 4 years. At La Silla during the winter season (April–August): minimum 8 years. During the summer season (September–March) both observatories welcome families with young children.
home.faq6q: What's the difference between Paranal and La Silla?
home.faq6aparanalDesc: hosts the VLT (Very Large Telescope) — four 8.2-metre telescopes operating as one instrument. In 2024 it detected 32 erupting protostars and photographed supernova SN 2024ggi just 26 hours after its detection. Up to 60 spots per visit. Operates on Saturdays in winter season; in summer the schedule varies according to the ESO team.
home.faq6asillaDesc: operates HARPS, the most precise spectrograph for detecting low-mass exoplanets. In 2025 it added SOXS, a next-generation instrument for studying rapid astrophysical transients. Up to 40 spots per visit. Operates on Saturdays in winter; in summer the schedule may open to additional days according to ESO availability.
home.faq7q: How do I get there? Is there official transport?
home.faq7a: ESO does not provide transport. Paranal is ~130 km south of Antofagasta on Route 1. La Silla is ~160 km north of La Serena. Your own vehicle is required. ESO sends detailed access instructions upon confirming your booking.
home.portalSubtitlePre: Manage everything from one place with your
home.portalSubtitlePost: code you received by email. No login, no password — just your code and the last 4 digits of your ID.
home.faqHelpPre: If you do not find your answer, use the contact form or the

meta.title: Contact | ESO Observatories Chile
meta.description: Contact the ESO Chile team for general enquiries or to request a group visit to La Silla or Paranal.

header.heading: How can we help you?
header.subtitle: Use the general enquiry form or request a visit for your group. We respond within 2 to 3 business days.

response.heading: Response time
response.body: The ESO team reviews messages Monday to Friday and responds within 2 to 3 business days.

groups.heading: Group visits
groups.body: For groups of 11 or more people (schools, universities, companies), select the Group booking tab in the form. Maximum capacity: 40 spots at La Silla and 60 spots at Paranal. Visits on Saturdays in winter; in summer the schedule is set by the ESO team. Download the participant template and attach it when replying to our email.

individual.body: For individual bookings of up to 10 people, use the booking system directly. It is faster and you get instant confirmation.

form.placeholderMensajeAdicional: Additional information you would like to share...
form.numPersonasHint: For groups up to 10 use the standard system.
form.downloadText: Download the participant template and attach it when replying to our email.
form.errorMensajeMin: The message must be at least 20 characters.
form.errorNumPersonas: Minimum 11 people. For smaller groups use the standard system.
form.errorFechaPref1: Please indicate at least one preferred date.
form.serverRateLimit: Too many messages. Wait a few minutes before trying again.
form.serverInvalid: Invalid data. Please review the form and try again.
form.serverGeneric: Could not send the message. Please try again.
form.serverConnection: Connection error. Check your internet and try again.
form.successBody: The ESO team will review it and reply within 2 to 3 business days to the email you provided.

acompanantes.successBody: Your companion list has been updated. Remember to confirm your attendance before the deadline.
acompanantes.errorNoCupos: No spots available to add more people to this tour

acompanantes.windowClosedBody: The modification deadline has passed. Only the ESO team can make changes. Write to reservas@observatorioseso.cl
acompanantes.cancelledBody: It is not possible to modify companions on a cancelled booking.
